focusPoints.label: What “good” feels like right now.
focusPoints.reason: What expectations and success bar feel most uncertain for him after three months in. Could be shifting requirements, unclear ownership, or just the normal dip—worth clarifying what specifically feels fuzzy.

focusPoints.label: How safe it feels to ask for help.
focusPoints.reason: Whether he feels connected to support and safe raising confusion or frustration. One possibility is he’s trying to prove himself by absorbing uncertainty rather than asking early.

focusPoints.label: Where the team setup makes it harder.
focusPoints.reason: If the role feels harder than it needs to be, this is the fastest way to find the friction points you can remove—process, review patterns, access to context, or how help is requested and received.

focusPoints.label: What he’s saying yes to versus no to.
focusPoints.reason: What’s pulling his attention between proving himself and the actual highest-leverage work. This helps separate “shifted requirements” from a broader mismatch in priorities or sequencing.

name: Kenji
notes: Kenji is around three months in, and this is a common point where uncertainty and confidence dips show up. I need to understand whether he feels clear on expectations, connected to support, and safe asking for help. Notes suggest he may be carrying frustration about shifting requirements while trying to prove himself. This should stay exploratory, not corrective: I want to hear what feels hardest right now and where our team setup is making the role harder than it needs to be.
